channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until Iranian-backed Houthi rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VA denies policy change after internal memo rescinds transgender veterans’ guidance
item.description: In an internal VA memo seen by NPR, the VA says it's rescinding a directive that contains detailed guidance on the kind of care transgender veterans can receive at VA facilities.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: US prepares to deport about 300 alleged gang members to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration will pay El Salvador $6 million to imprison for one year about 300 alleged members of the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang, in one of the first instances of the Central American country taking migrants from the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz hits road to rip Trump policies as he works to keep his own national profile up
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz, the 2024 Democratic vice presidential nominee, held forums in Iowa and Nebraska. He is using them to dissect Trump administration actions and perhaps boost his own standing in the soul-searching Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: President Trump signs continuing resolution, officially averting a shutdown
item.description: A government shutdown has officially been averted, as President Donald Trump signs the continuing resolution into law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs a bill funding the government for 6 months, avoiding a shutdown
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed into law legislation funding the government through the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Transgender sailors, Marines offered benefits to voluntarily leave service or face being kicked out
item.description: The policy aligns with an executive order President Donald Trump signed in January to bar transgender individuals from serving in the military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats to hold 'Day of Action' to push back against GOP-backed spending bill
item.description: House Democrats will hold a "Day of Action" across the country on Tuesday to push back against the GOP-backed spending bill to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Injunction lifted on Trump executive orders slashing federal DEI support
item.description: An appeals court on Friday overturned a previous judge's decision to block President Donald Trump's executive orders surrounding diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma schools chief won’t abandon classroom Bible effort
item.description: Last year, Ryan Walters issued a mandate for schools to incorporate lessons on the Bible in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Nessen, press secretary to President Ford, dies at 90
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ron Nessen, a veteran broadcast journalist who was press secretary for President Gerald Ford and sought to restore the integrity that the position had lost during the Nixon administration, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: DOUGLASS K. DANIEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Infighting to Irrelevance
item.description: The current Democratic infighting and political malpractice over forcing or not forcing a government shutdown exposes a leaderless party that believes in opposing Donald Trump and issues that 80% of Americans agree with.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump environmental rollbacks would boost pollution and endanger lives, former EPA heads say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three former Environmental Protection Agency leaders sounded an alarm Friday, saying rollbacks proposed by EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin endanger the lives of millions of Americans and abandon the agency's dual mission to protect the environment and human health.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Spending bill to avert shutdown successfully beats filibuster with hours until deadline
item.description: The Senate voted on a key procedural motion regarding the House-passed stopgap spending bill on Friday. If a measure is not passed by midnight, the government will enter a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As 'match day' nears, med schools, lawmakers want to expand residencies to keep doctors in Nevada
item.description: On Friday, March 21 — just a week away — more than 40,000 medical students around the country will find out where they will be serving their residencies. About 300 of those graduates are from UNLV, UNR and Touro University, but there aren't enough residencies in the state to keep them here.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer backing budget bill a 'slap in the face': AOC
item.description: She called the Senate minority leader's intent to vote for a GOP-backed spending bill a "slap in the face.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Proposal seeks to safeguard Nevada bus drivers from passenger assaults
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would require public transit companies to install safety barriers and panic buttons amid an uptick in driver assaults. Democratic Assem.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security says it will no longer accept bank changes over the phone
item.description: Those who need to change their direct deposit information with Social Security will have to do it online or in-person.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of fired federal workers must be offered reinstatement, a judge rules
item.description: Thousands of probationary federal employees fired by the Trump administration must be offered job reinstatement, a judge in San Francisco has ruled, because they were terminated unlawfully.
item.dc:creator: Chris Arnold and Emily Feng, MPR News

item.title: White House withdraws nomination of David Weldon to run the CDC
item.description: Dr. Dave Weldon, Trump's pick for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, was withdrawn from consideration shortly before a scheduled Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House bill to encourage sharing of immigration data with ICE introduced
item.description: The bill aims to prevent state and local governments from limiting their employees’ ability to share immigration data, including the legal status of immigrants, with federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tax
item.description: The European Commission is calling on President Donald Trump to revoke tariffs now that he's threatened a 200 percent tariff on European wine, Champagne and spirits if Europe goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge cancels hearing in NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – A hearing in the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has been canceled for Friday. U.S. District Judge Dale Ho, who's weighing the Justice Department's request to dismiss the charges against Adams, called the hearing off.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Digital license plate bill could include a way for Nevadans to pay registration in installments
item.description: GPS features, no stickers and possibly a way to pay your vehicle registration in installments. Those were some of the reasons that digital license plates might get support at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UNLV, UNR funding would dip under new formula presented to Nevada lawmakers
item.description: A rebuilt funding formula for the state's colleges and universities became a target for officials Tuesday during a budget hearing in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
